description: When a consumer fetches a `data_refs[].location` over HTTPS, an HTTP redirect whose target authority (host + port) differs from the original request's authority MUST be refused — see RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.9. A cooperating attacker server can pass the initial IP-range filter on its own public IP and then issue a 30x redirect to an internal address; the same-authority redirect rule blocks this. Total redirects MUST also be capped at 3 follows, and a same-authority redirect MUST reuse the pinned IP rather than triggering a fresh DNS lookup.
ssrf_rule: RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.9 (DataRef location SSRF protection — same-authority redirect cap)

input.scenario: Consumer fetches https://data.example.com/file; the server returns a 30x whose Location header points to a different authority

expected.behavior: The consumer MUST refuse to follow the cross-authority redirect. The check MUST be a string comparison on the authority (host AND port) component of the URL. Path/query changes within the same authority MAY be followed up to the cap of 3 follows; a same-authority redirect MUST reuse the pinned IP and MUST NOT trigger a fresh DNS resolution. The DataRef MUST be treated as unfetchable and unverifiable.
expected.rationale: Without same-authority enforcement, an attacker who controls a public-facing host can use it as a bounce point to any internal address, bypassing the IP-range filter that ran only on the initial DNS resolution.
